steps.title: Use the exterior angle
steps.content:
Angle $ACD$ is an exterior angle, so it equals the sum of the two remote interior angles:
$$x+y=(4x+8)+(5x+4)$$
$$y=8x+12$$

steps.title: Use the triangle sum
steps.content:
The interior angles of triangle $ABC$ sum to $180^{\circ}$:
$$(4x+8)+(5x+4)+(5y+10)=180$$
$$9x+5y=158$$

steps.title: Solve for the requested sum
steps.content:
Substitute $y=8x+12$ into the triangle-sum equation and solve:
$$9x+5(8x+12)=158$$
$$49x=98$$
$$x=2$$
$$y=28$$
$$x+y=30$$The answer is 30.
